NIST Awards $15 Million for Quantum Computing Research

March 4, 2024

The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) today announced $15 million in new grants for quantum computing research under the CHIPS and Science Act. The grants will be distributed across five universities over a three-year period.

The research will focus on quantum error correction, scalable qubit architectures, and quantum networking technologies. NIST Director said these investments are critical for maintaining US leadership in quantum information science.
